title: Your Life in a Cartel at Every Rank (No Way Out)
hook: Dawn, at your own gate. The dogs stopped barking an hour ago — that was the first sign. Your phone went dead at four, and not one of your two hundred gunmen answered, because they already know how this ends.
body: A cautionary, anti-glamour POV climb through every rank of a cartel — you start as a poor kid on a dust-town corner and rise to the man in the walled compound, and learn the only two exits were always a cell or a grave. You live it in second person, present tense, level by level: EL HALCÓN, the thirteen-year-old lookout with a phone and a whistle for six hundred dollars a month → EL BURRERO, the mule riding the sierra with packages you're told never to open → EL SICARIO, the gunman they pay eighty-five dollars a body, the cheapest, most disposable thing in the whole machine → JEFE DE PLAZA, the boss of a corridor taxing every truck and every migrant who crosses your ground → EL LUGARTENIENTE, the lieutenant who runs the war and stops reading the dead as names → EL PATRÓN, the billion-dollar kingpin in a mountain fortress with a tunnel under the bed → LA RED, the network so big that taking you down doesn't move the price of a kilo for a single day → and the level above the kingpin: LA DEMANDA, the buyers up north who spend a hundred and fifty billion dollars a year and never once learn your name. You climb from being the cheapest thing in the machine to running it, and find out you were inventory the whole way — and inventory gets restocked. Real, non-instructional facts, dramatized: 'plata o plomo,' el piso, sicario pay a poverty wage, a real kingpin listed near a billion, and a drug war that has disappeared a hundred thousand people. Watch the whole series — every life, every level. Dramatization for educational and entertainment purposes; characters are fictional, no methods are disclosed, and nothing here glamorizes the trade — it costs everyone everything.
pinned: You climb from the corner to the compound and still end with nothing — at which level would YOU have stopped, if the only way out was a cell or a grave?
poll: Where would this life have ended you? The corner / The first body / The war / The tunnel under the bed
